feed.title: Johannesson Lab
feed.subtitle: The lab group of professor Hanna Johannesson at Stockholm University

entry.title: Shalya and Matilde join the lab

link.title: Shalya and Matilde join the lab

entry.content: Today we welcome two new PhD-students - Matilde Fonseca and Shalya Moodley - who start in our group today, recruited on the ERC Advanced Grant ”FUNGERM” - on the Early Sequestered Germline of Fungi.
entry.summary: Today we welcome two new PhD-students - Matilde Fonseca and Shalya Moodley - who start in our group today, recruited on the ERC Advanced Grant ”FUNGERM” - on the Early Sequestered Germline of Fungi.

entry.title: Chris defends their master thesis
entry.content: Chris presents their internship focusing on the histone variation in the Fungi. Impressing bioinformatic work exploring available genomes for unknown histone variants in Dikarya. Thanks for spending a fall and winter with us in Stockholm, and enlightening us on the topic!
entry.summary: Chris presents their internship focusing on the histone variation in the Fungi. Impressing bioinformatic work exploring available genomes for unknown histone variants in Dikarya. Thanks for spending a fall and winter with us in Stockholm, and enlightening us on the topic!

entry.title: Marisol joins the lab
entry.content: Today, Marisol starts as a research engineer in our group. We are so happy to have her joining us, we look forward to much joy in researching fungal evolution together.
entry.summary: Today, Marisol starts as a research engineer in our group. We are so happy to have her joining us, we look forward to much joy in researching fungal evolution together.

entry.title: Hanna visits Linnaeus University in Kalmar

link.title: Hanna visits Linnaeus University in Kalmar

entry.content: Hanna went to Kalmar to serve in the committee of Danilo Di Leu, and got the chance to see the interesting work done in Kalmar and to give an informal talk about the germline sequestration in Marasmius.
entry.summary: Hanna went to Kalmar to serve in the committee of Danilo Di Leu, and got the chance to see the interesting work done in Kalmar and to give an informal talk about the germline sequestration in Marasmius.

entry.title: Rosa defends her master thesis
entry.content: Today Rosa van Zandvoort successfully defended her master thesis “The impact of Marasmius oreades fairy rings on the soil microbiome and physicochemical properties”! It turned out to be an ambitious and comprehensive study, including all from study design, field-sampling, different types of laboratory work, data crunching, interpretations and writing. Definitely worth a masters-degree! Congratulations, Rosa!
entry.summary: Today Rosa van Zandvoort successfully defended her master thesis “The impact of Marasmius oreades fairy rings on the soil microbiome and physicochemical properties”! It turned out to be an ambitious and comprehensive study, including all from study design, field-sampling, different types of laboratory work, data crunching, interpretations and writing. Definitely worth a masters-degree! Congratulations, Rosa!

entry.title: Noah successfully defended their thesis
entry.content: Today, Noah successfully defended their PhD-thesis! It was a great discussion on phylogomics and fungal adaptation with the opponent Antonis Rokas from Vanderbilt university. Congratulations and well done, Noah!
entry.summary: Today, Noah successfully defended their PhD-thesis! It was a great discussion on phylogomics and fungal adaptation with the opponent Antonis Rokas from Vanderbilt university. Congratulations and well done, Noah!

entry.title: Minisymposium on Fungal Evolution at DEEP

link.title: Minisymposium on Fungal Evolution at DEEP

entry.content: One day before the PhD-defense by Noah Hensen, we arranged a minisymposium on Fungal Evolution, which started with a talk by Noah’s opponent, Antonis Rokas, and was followed by talks by Inger Skrede (Oslo), Jennifer Anderson (SLU, Uppsala), Marion Orsucci form our own group, and Rike Stelkens (Zoology, SU). It was a really great event with Vivi Täckholm-salen full of audience!
entry.summary: One day before the PhD-defense by Noah Hensen, we arranged a minisymposium on Fungal Evolution, which started with a talk by Noah’s opponent, Antonis Rokas, and was followed by talks by Inger Skrede (Oslo), Jennifer Anderson (SLU, Uppsala), Marion Orsucci form our own group, and Rike Stelkens (Zoology, SU). It was a really great event with Vivi Täckholm-salen full of audience!

entry.title: Noah nails their thesis
entry.content: Today, Noah ”nailed” their thesis at the entrance of the Department. One milestone achieved for Noah, and another step towards completing the PhD. After nailing, we had a nice fika in the DEEP lunchroom.
entry.summary: Today, Noah ”nailed” their thesis at the entrance of the Department. One milestone achieved for Noah, and another step towards completing the PhD. After nailing, we had a nice fika in the DEEP lunchroom.

entry.title: Thibault leaves the lab
entry.content: We said farewell to Thibault today, with a nice fika in the DEEP kitchen. We will miss him in the group, and wish him good luck for future adventures
entry.summary: We said farewell to Thibault today, with a nice fika in the DEEP kitchen. We will miss him in the group, and wish him good luck for future adventures

entry.title: The end of the semester
entry.content: Today, we did a full day of discussions of science and an evaluation of our work-environment. To also acknowledge the completion of a fruitful semester, we went on a guided tour at Edvard Anderssons växthus and enjoyed a great lunch at Gamla Orangeriet.
entry.summary: Today, we did a full day of discussions of science and an evaluation of our work-environment. To also acknowledge the completion of a fruitful semester, we went on a guided tour at Edvard Anderssons växthus and enjoyed a great lunch at Gamla Orangeriet.
